content.problemStatement:
Write a C program that calculates and prints the power of a number.

You are required to implement a function named `power` that takes two integers as arguments:

base:base:base:  the number to be raised

exponent:exponent:exponent: the power to which the base is raised

The function should return the result of raising basebasebase to the power of exponentexponentexponent.


Input Format

A single line containing two integers: basebasebase and exponentexponentexponent


Output Format

Print a single integer representing basebasebase raised to the power of exponentexponentexponent
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two space-separated integers: base and exponent.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the integer value of base raised to the power of exponent.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Any number to the power 0 is 1.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using the concept of exponentiation by squaring, also known as fast power. This method reduces the time complexity of calculating the power of a number from O(n) to O(log n).
solution.timeComplexity: O(log n) per test case, O(T log n) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
